textOverlays.text: Hi Anthony, just checking in. How's your sleep and energy during your day been the past week?
I started taking Magnesium glycinate like we talked about and noticed I've been falling asleep faster. But also feeling a little groggy the next day.
Let's tweak your dosage down to 100mg elemental and only 3 to 4 days out of the week.
I'll be sure to remind you around 2 to 3 hours before you sleep starting tonight.
Now that you've mentioned it, I have noticed feeling a bit low energy lately.
Your ferritin was 33 ng/mL which is borderline low. Could definitely explain your low stamina grogginess too. Have you been keeping up with the iron supplements like we discussed?
I've looped in your clinician. He suggests retesting your nutrient markers and checking if your iron stores have improved.
We can see if we need to shift your action plan and/or supplement stack.
Here's a custom panel for you to book:
Mito - Advanced diagnostics. Personalized health plans. (dentro de um cartão)

textOverlays.text: What's keeping your brain awake at night
Does your mind race with worries and to-do lists the moment your head hits the pillow?
This state of hyperarousal – driven by stress hormones – can sabotage your sleep, increase inflammation, and accelerate aging.
Here are some ways to retrain your mind and body to relax for deeper sleep:

analysis.lookAndFeel: O slide tem uma atmosfera calma, cinematográfica e informativa. O visual é sereno e esteticamente agradável, contrastando com o texto que descreve a ansiedade noturna. A iluminação suave e a paleta de cores escuras com um toque quente (#F4E8D1) e frio (#1C1C2B) criam uma sensação de intimidade e realismo noturno. A tipografia limpa sobre a imagem confere um tom de autoridade e bem-estar, posicionando a marca como uma solução baseada na ciência. A intenção é criar empatia ao descrever um problema comum (a 'causa raiz') antes de apresentar soluções nos slides seguintes.

A paleta de cores é composta por azuis escuros (#1C1C2B), tons de pele quentes (#A18981) e brancos suaves da roupa de cama (#FFFFFF). O clima é sereno, calmo e autêntico. textOverlays.text:
②
Nutrition and diet strategies

A balanced dinner – protein, healthy fats, and complex carbs – helps maintain stable glucose levels into the night.

A modest serving of carbohydrates can help usher tryptophan (the precursor to serotonin and melatonin) into the brain.

Vitamin B6, magnesium, zinc, and calcium all support neurotransmitter production for sleep.

textOverlays.text: Mind-body approaches to quiet the brain
Studies show brief daily mindfulness sessions reduce hyperarousal and cut pre-sleep “mind racing” in chronic insomniacs, improving overall sleep quality.
Simple breathing practices — like 4-7-8 or slow diaphragmatic breathing — activate your parasympathetic (rest-and-digest) nervous system.

textOverlays.text:
Erratic bedtimes unsettle your internal clock. Aim for a stable bedtime/wake time, even on weekends, to reinforce a strong circadian rhythm.

Regular exercise lowers stress and deepens sleep, but intense workouts too close to bedtime can elevate core temperature and adrenaline. Finish vigorous sessions at least an hour or two before lights-out.

analysis.lookAndFeel: O mood é inspirador, calmo e cinematográfico. O uso de uma silhueta contra um pôr-do-sol cria uma sensação de foco e disciplina, mas de forma serena e não agressiva. A paleta de cores quentes é reconfortante e alinha-se com o tema do descanso e do ritmo circadiano. Como sexto slide, serve como um ponto de conselho prático, usando uma imagem poderosa e motivacional para ilustrar o conceito de 'ajustes de estilo de vida' relacionados com o exercício.

A paleta de cores do céu é quente e analógica, com laranjas profundos, rosas e castanhos: #A46A60, #D0876F. O mood é calmo, focado e inspirador. textOverlays.text:
High cortisol late in the evening can leave you feeling 'wired but tired.' A 4-point daily cortisol test shows whether your stress hormone curve stays elevated instead of dropping at bedtime.

Chronic low-grade inflammation often correlates with poor sleep. Elevated hs-CRP can signal that your body's inflammatory load is taxing your nervous system and fueling nighttime wake-ups.

But his girlfriend refused to accept that.
She sent one last desperate email—to a doctor using AI to uncover hidden treatments in existing medicines.

textOverlays.text:
Insulin is an early warning sign of metabolic dysfunction—often going out of range before glucose levels do. Elevated insulin is closely tied to insulin resistance and increased all-cause mortality.

To lower it, focus on caloric restriction, weight loss, regular exercise, and a low-carb, high-fat diet.

Support may include supplements like ALA, magnesium, and berberine, or prescriptions such as GLP-1s, Metformin, and SGLT2 inhibitors.

textOverlays.text:
HDL-C, often called the “good cholesterol,” helps remove excess cholesterol, reduce inflammation, support metabolic health, and protect brain function. Low HDL levels are linked to a 49% increase in all-cause mortality.

To raise it, incorporate HIIT and strength training, reduce sugar, and eat more monounsaturated fats, omega-3s, and polyphenol-rich foods like dark chocolate and berries. Supplements such as omega-3s, curcumin, and astaxanthin can help, along with prescriptions like fibrates, statins, and PCSK9 inhibitors.
